name: read_from_mem_tso_equals_core_mem_read
reason: `read_from_mem_tso` should unfold to `core_mem(core).read(addr)` which is `pt_mem.write_seq(sbuf[core]).read(addr)` — tests definitional unfolding consistency

name: flags_combine_absorbs_supervisor
code:
proof fn phi_2_flags_combine_absorbs_supervisor(f: Flags)
    requires
        f.is_supervisor,
    ensures
        forall|g: Flags| f.combine(g).is_supervisor,
{
}
reason: Once `is_supervisor` is true, combining with any flags keeps it true (OR absorbs true) — this means a supervisor-only directory entry makes the entire subtree supervisor-only, potentially too restrictive

name: flags_combine_restricts_writable
code:
proof fn phi_3_flags_combine_restricts_writable(f: Flags)
    requires
        !f.is_writable,
    ensures
        forall|g: Flags| !f.combine(g).is_writable,
{
}
reason: Once `is_writable` is false, combining with any flags keeps it false (AND absorbs false) — a read-only directory makes the entire subtree read-only regardless of leaf permissions

name: view_external_body_unconstrained
code:
proof fn phi_4_view_external_body_unconstrained(mem1: PTMem, mem2: PTMem)
    requires
        mem1.mem === mem2.mem,
        mem1.pml4 == mem2.pml4,
    ensures
        mem1@ === mem2@,
{
}
reason: `PTMem::view` is `external_body` — structural equality of inputs should imply equal outputs, but this is trusted without proof; the PTE interpretation could be nondeterministic

name: lemma_write_seq_idle_external_body_direct
reason: `write_seq(seq![])` should be identity by fold_left base case — tests that even the trivial case (empty writes) preserves reads without needing the external_body lemma
